ExportSettings.Notes: Snapshot export configuration. Enabled = enable snapshot exports. OutputDirectory = single directory (legacy, use OutputDirectories instead). OutputDirectories = array of directories to write snapshots to (supports local paths and UNC paths). FileNamePattern = filename template with {ServerName}, {Timestamp:format}, {SnapshotId} placeholders. ExportIntervals: IntervalMinutes = export every N minutes, OnAlertTrigger = export when alert is generated, OnDemand = allow manual export via API. Retention: MaxAgeHours = delete files older than N hours, MaxFileCount = keep only N most recent files, CompressionEnabled = compress JSON files to .gz after export.

Alerting.Notes: Alert distribution configuration. Enabled = enable alert distribution. Channels = array of alert delivery channels. Each channel: Type = SMS/Email/EventLog/File/WKMonitor, Enabled = enable this channel, MinSeverity = minimum severity (Informational/Warning/Critical) to send, Settings = channel-specific configuration. Throttling: Enabled = enable alert throttling/deduplication, MaxAlertsPerHour = global limit, WarningSuppressionMinutes/ErrorSuppressionMinutes/InformationalSuppressionMinutes = how long to suppress duplicate alerts by severity. SuppressedChannels = global list of channel types to suppress for all alerts (e.g., ["SMS", "Email"]). CategorySuppressions = per-category channel suppression (e.g., {"Processor": ["SMS"], "Memory": ["Email", "SMS"]}). Channel types must match Type in Channels. If empty, no suppression is applied. Alert-specific SuppressedChannels are merged with config-based suppression.

Channels.Notes: SMS alert channel via PSWin API. Enabled = enable SMS alerts. MinSeverity = minimum severity to send SMS. Settings: ApiUrl = SMS gateway endpoint, Client/Password = authentication, Sender = sender ID, DefaultCountryCode = prefix for numbers without country code, MaxMessageLength = truncate messages to N characters. Recipients are managed via NotificationRecipients.json (people array with schedule-based availability).

Windows Event Log alert channel. Enabled = write alerts to Windows Event Log. MinSeverity = minimum severity to log. Settings: empty (uses default Application log). Alerts are written to Windows Event Viewer under Application log with source 'ServerMonitor'.

File-based alert logging. Enabled = write alerts to log file. MinSeverity = minimum severity to log. Settings: LogPath = file path with {Date} placeholder (replaced with yyyyMMdd format). All alerts at or above MinSeverity are appended to the file with timestamps.

Email alert channel via SMTP. Enabled = enable email alerts. MinSeverity = minimum severity to send email. Settings: SmtpServer/SmtpPort = SMTP server configuration, From = sender address (supports {ComputerName} placeholder), EnableSsl = use SSL/TLS encryption, Username/Password = authentication (leave empty for anonymous/Windows auth). Recipients are managed via NotificationRecipients.json (people array with schedule-based availability).

WKMonitor integration (legacy monitoring system). Enabled = write alerts to WKMonitor file format. MinSeverity = minimum severity to send. Settings: ProductionPath = UNC path for production environment, TestPath = UNC path for test environment (selected based on DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT), ProgramName = program identifier in WKMonitor, ForceAll = send all alerts regardless of throttling (default: false = respect throttling).

Throttling.Notes: Alert throttling and deduplication. Enabled = enable throttling to prevent alert spam. MaxAlertsPerHour = global limit across all channels (prevents overwhelming recipients). WarningSuppressionMinutes = suppress duplicate Warning alerts for N minutes. ErrorSuppressionMinutes = suppress duplicate Error/Critical alerts for N minutes. InformationalSuppressionMinutes = suppress duplicate Informational alerts for N minutes. Duplicate detection normalizes alert messages (strips numeric values) to catch similar alerts.

General.Notes: General application settings. ServerName is always auto-detected from Environment.MachineName (system ComputerName). MonitoringEnabled controls all monitors. DataRetentionHours affects snapshot cleanup.

MemoryManagement.Notes: Automatic memory cleanup for in-memory collections. CleanupAgeHours = remove items older than N hours. MaxAlertsInMemory = cap on alert count. CleanupIntervalMinutes = how often to run cleanup. Applies to: Alerts, ExternalEvents, ProcessCpuTracker, AlertManager tracking.

Runtime.Notes: Runtime control settings. AutoShutdownTime (HH:mm format) = scheduled shutdown. MaxRuntimeHours = max duration before auto-shutdown. TestTimeoutSeconds = exit after N seconds (for testing). ConfigReloadIntervalMinutes = check for config changes every N minutes. CommonAppsettingsFile = UNC path to common config file (appsettings.ServerMonitorAgent.json) - if set, syncs to local appsettings.json when hash differs. NotificationRecipients.json is expected in the same folder. DevMode = when true, skips config sync (useful for local development/testing). All null = run indefinitely.

RestApi.Notes: REST API configuration. Enabled = start HTTP server. Port = listening port. EnableSwagger = interactive API docs at /swagger. AutoConfigureFirewall = add firewall rule automatically. Example address: http://localhost:8999 (Swagger UI: http://localhost:8999/swagger).

PerformanceScaling.Notes: Performance scaling for low-capacity servers. When the server name matches LowCapacityServerPattern (regex), all PollingIntervalSeconds and IntervalMinutes values are multiplied by IntervalMultiplier, and all startup delays are multiplied by StartupDelayMultiplier. This reduces resource usage on servers with limited CPU/RAM.
PerformanceScaling.LowCapacityServerPatternExamples: Regex patterns: '-app$' (ends with -app), '-app|-web' (ends with -app or -web), '.*app.*' (contains app)

ProcessorMonitoring.Notes: CPU monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = how often to check. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent trigger alerts if exceeded for SustainedDurationSeconds. Alerts: WarningAlertSeverity/CriticalAlertSeverity = severity level for threshold alerts (Informational/Warning/Critical). PerCoreMonitoring = track each CPU core separately. TrackTopProcesses = number of top CPU-consuming processes to track.

MemoryMonitoring.Notes: Physical RAM monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent trigger alerts if exceeded for SustainedDurationSeconds. Alerts: WarningAlertSeverity/CriticalAlertSeverity = severity level for threshold alerts (Informational/Warning/Critical). TrackTopProcesses = number of top memory-consuming processes to track.

VirtualMemoryMonitoring.Notes: Virtual memory (page file) monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent for virtual memory usage. SustainedDurationSeconds = how long threshold must be exceeded. Alerts: WarningAlertSeverity/CriticalAlertSeverity = severity level (Informational/Warning/Critical).

DiskUsageMonitoring.Notes: Disk I/O performance monitoring (queue length, response time). PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. DisksToMonitor = specific drives to monitor. Thresholds: MaxQueueLength/MaxResponseTimeMs trigger alerts if exceeded for SustainedDurationSeconds. Alerts: AlertSeverity = severity level for I/O alerts (Informational/Warning/Critical).

DiskSpaceMonitoring.Notes: Disk space (free/used) monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. DisksToMonitor: ["*"] = auto-detect all local fixed drives. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent trigger alerts when usage exceeds. MinimumFreeSpaceGB = absolute minimum free space threshold. Alerts: WarningAlertSeverity/CriticalAlertSeverity = severity level (Informational/Warning/Critical).

NetworkMonitoring.Notes: Network connectivity monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = how often to ping/check hosts. BaselineHosts = list of hosts to monitor. Alerts: ConnectivityLostSeverity/HighLatencySeverity/PacketLossSeverity/PortUnreachableSeverity = severity level for each alert type (Informational/Warning/Critical).

UptimeMonitoring.Notes: System uptime monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. Alerts: UnexpectedRebootAlert = enable unexpected reboot detection. MaximumUptimeDaysWarning = alert if uptime exceeds N days. Severity settings: UnexpectedRebootSeverity/ExcessiveUptimeSeverity/DirtyShutdownSeverity (Informational/Warning/Critical).

WindowsUpdateMonitoring.Notes: Windows Update status monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. Thresholds: MaxPendingSecurityUpdates/MaxPendingCriticalUpdates = alert threshold. MaxDaysSinceLastUpdate = days threshold. Alerts: SecurityUpdateAlertSeverity/CriticalUpdateAlertSeverity/DaysSinceLastUpdateSeverity = alert level (Informational/Warning/Critical).

EventMonitoring.Notes: Windows Event Log monitoring using real-time EventLogWatcher. Events are detected immediately when they occur (no polling delay). EventsToMonitor = array of events to watch. Each event: Enabled = enable/disable this specific event check, EventId = Windows event ID, Source/LogName = event source and log name, Level = severity (Informational/Warning/Error/Critical), MaxOccurrences = alert if event occurs more than N times in TimeWindowMinutes (MaxOccurrences = 0 means alert on any occurrence). TimeWindowMinutes = time window to track occurrences.

EventsToMonitor.Description: System - Kernel-Power (system rebooted without cleanly shutting down)
Task Scheduler - Task failed to start
Task Scheduler - Task completed with errors
DCOM - Server did not register with DCOM
Services - Service terminated with service-specific error
Services - Boot-start or system-start driver failed to load
System - Low virtual memory condition detected
IIS - App pool disabled by rapid-fail protection
IIS - App pool cannot start (configuration error)
IIS - App pool auto-disabled after repeated failures
IIS - Worker process crash (fatal communication error)
IIS - App pool auto-disabled (job object limit exceeded)
IIS - App pool requesting recycle (configuration change)
IIS - App pool requesting recycle (private memory limit exceeded)
IIS - App pool timed out during shutdown
IIS - Worker process failed to initialize
IIS - W3SVC service could not bind to port

ScheduledTaskMonitoring.Notes: Windows Scheduled Task monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. Alerts: DisabledTaskSeverity/FailedTaskSeverity/MissedRunSeverity/OverdueTaskSeverity = severity level for each alert type (Informational/Warning/Critical). TasksToMonitor = array of tasks to monitor.

TasksToMonitor.Description: All Tasks owned by the same user as the ServerMonitor tool

Db2DiagMonitoring.Notes: IBM DB2 diagnostic log monitoring. PatternsToMonitor defines actions: Skip (ignore completely), LogOnly (log to SqlErrorLogPath but no alert), Remap/Escalate (change severity), Keep (original behavior). Unmatched entries use original behavior (alerts for Error/Warning levels).
Db2DiagMonitoring.ServerNamePatternExamples: Regex patterns: '-db$' (ends with -db), '^db-' (starts with db-), '.*db.*' (contains db), 'p-no1fkmrap-db|t-no1inltst-db' (specific servers)
Db2DiagMonitoring.Db2MinimumLogLevelNotes: Minimum DB2 log level to read from db2diag.log. DB2 levels: Critical, Severe, Error, Warning, Info. Default: Error (reads Critical, Severe, Error).
Db2DiagMonitoring.MinimumAlertSeverityNotes: Minimum agent alert severity to generate (applied AFTER pattern remapping). Agent severities: Critical, Warning, Informational. Default: Warning (suppresses Informational alerts).
Db2DiagMonitoring.MaxLogFileSizeBytesNotes: Maximum log file size in bytes (314572800 = 300 MB). Files larger than this are skipped with a warning alert to prevent out-of-memory issues.
Db2DiagMonitoring.SqlErrorLogPathNotes: Path for LogOnly/AlsoLogToFile entries. Placeholders: {ServerName} = machine name, {Date} = yyyyMMdd, {Database} = database name.

PatternsToMonitor.Description: Self Tuning Memory Manager automatic configuration changes
Access Plan Manager package cache resize operations
Automatic configuration parameter adjustments by STMM
New diagnostic log file started (log rotation)
Foreign key constraint violation (expected in federated operations)
Deadlock or timeout - transient, auto-retried by application
Processing cancelled by user or timeout
DRDA wrapper probe:10 errors (federated SQL execution, high volume expected)
DRDA wrapper errors (standard severity federated issues)
PatternsToMonitor.MessageTemplate: DRDA Error in {Database}: federated query issue

PatternsToMonitor.Description: User interrupt detected (SQLD_INTRP) - session killed or cancelled, expected during batch maintenance
SQLCA already initialized (ZRC=0x8012006D) - transient, auto-recovered by DB2
Critical internal DB2 failure (negative ZRC codes)
Query Gateway QPLEX connection user mapping error
PatternsToMonitor.Regex: Query Gateway.*sqlqgGetUserOptions|can't get valid connected user name.*QPLEX
PatternsToMonitor.MessageTemplate: [{Instance}] [{Database}] QPLEX user mapping error for user {AuthId}

PatternsToMonitor.Description: No saved session environment to restore - benign DB2 internal condition after session interrupt
PatternsToMonitor.Regex: No saved session env to restore

Db2InstanceMonitoring.Notes: DB2 instance monitoring via MON_GET_* functions. Collects session counts, long-running queries, blocking sessions, and today's diag summary. Only active on servers matching ServerNamePattern.
Db2InstanceMonitoring.ServerNamePatternExamples: Regex patterns: '-db$' (ends with -db), '^db-' (starts with db-), '.*db.*' (contains db)
Db2InstanceMonitoring.RefreshIntervalSecondsNotes: How often to refresh data (1200 = 20 minutes). Cached between refreshes.
Db2InstanceMonitoring.InstanceNamesNotes: DB2 instance names to monitor. Empty array means auto-detect from DB2INSTANCE env var.

Thresholds.LongRunningQueryThresholdSecondsNotes: Minimum elapsed seconds to display a query as 'long-running'.
Thresholds.LongRunningQueryWarningSecondsNotes: Elapsed seconds to generate a Warning alert (300 = 5 min).
Thresholds.LongRunningQueryCriticalSecondsNotes: Elapsed seconds to generate a Critical alert (1800 = 30 min).
Thresholds.LockWaitWarningSecondsNotes: Elapsed seconds for a lock wait to generate Warning alert.
Thresholds.LockWaitCriticalSecondsNotes: Elapsed seconds for a lock wait to generate Critical alert (300 = 5 min).
Thresholds.SessionCountWarningThresholdNotes: Generate Warning if total sessions exceed this value.
Thresholds.DiagErrorsTodayWarningThresholdNotes: Generate Warning if today's diag Error count exceeds this value.

Alerts.MaxOccurrencesNotes: Alert after N occurrences in TimeWindowMinutes (0 = every occurrence).

IisMonitoring.Notes: IIS monitoring via Microsoft.Web.Administration. Collects sites, app pools, worker processes. Only active on servers matching ServerNamePattern.
IisMonitoring.ServerNamePatternNotes: Regex pattern: '-app$' (servers ending with -app). Set empty to monitor on all servers.
IisMonitoring.WorkerProcessMemoryThresholdMBNotes: Alert if a worker process exceeds this private memory threshold (0 = disable).

SelfMonitoring.Notes: Self-monitoring of ServerMonitor.exe process health. If memory usage exceeds MemoryThresholdMB, generates a Critical alert immediately and gracefully shuts down after ShutdownDelaySeconds. At 2x threshold (6 GB), the process exits immediately without delay.
SelfMonitoring.MemoryThresholdMBNotes: Memory usage threshold in MB (3072 = 3 GB). Process generates Critical alert and shuts down if exceeded.
